LAW AND MOTION 302, RULING - A R G U E D; THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING. PLAINTIFF FIRST AMERICAN TITLE INSURANCE COMPANY'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION IS GRANTED AS TO ISSUES 1, 3, 5, AND 9 AND MOOT AS TO THE REMAINING ISSUES. DEFENDANT CANNOT ESTABLISH THE CAUSES OF ACTION FOR BREACH OF CONTRACT OR BAD FAITH OR THE REQUEST FOR PUNITIVE DAMAGES. IN ADDITION, DEFENDANT IS NOT ENTITLED TO A DECLARATION THAT IT IS OWED FURTHER POLICY BENEFITS BECAUSE THE UNDISPUTED FACTS DEMONSTRATE THE CONTRARY. THE UNDISPUTED EVIDENCE DEMONSTRATES THAT (1) DEFENDANT WAS LEGALLY OBLIGATED TO PAY OUT THE UNDISBURSED FUNDS TO THE CONTRACTORS; AND (2) THE UNDISBURSED FUNDS EXCEEDED THE AMOUNT THAT PLAINTIFF ULTIMATELY PAID THE CONTRACTORS. POLICY EXCLUSION 3(A) APPLIES BECAUSE DEFENDANT "CREATED" OR "SUFFERED" THE LIEN BY FAILING TO PAY THE UNDISBURSED FUNDS. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: HAROLD KAHN; COURT REPORTER: SHERRY SAWYER, CSR # 5976
NOTICE OF ENTRY OF ORDER/NOTICE OF RULING FILED GRANTING MTN FOE SUMM ADJ FILED BY PLAINTIFF FIRST AMERICAN TITLE INSURANCE COMPANY
DISMISSAL WITH PREJUDICE OF CROSS COMPLAINT FILED BY 1977 SERVICES CORPORATION ON MAR-15-2011.

Off Calendar Aug-09-2012 Continued To Oct-15-12 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 206

MOTION TO CONTINUE JURY TRIAL
Off Calendar Aug-09-2012 Text Ruling

MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION
Off Calendar Jul-13-2012 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES - ATTY. E.J. CHIARELLI OF CHIARELLI & MULLICA, TEL. NO. (925) 262-4888 APPEARING FOR THE PLAINTIFF;
ATTY. EDWARD SMITH OF THE STEYER LAW FIRM, TEL. NO. 421-3400 APPEARING FOR THE PLAINTIFF;
ATTY. THOMAS E. ALBORG OF THE ALBORG, VEILUVA & MARTIN LLP, TEL. NO. (925) 939-9880 APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANT.

RULING - A R G U E D; The Court adopted its tentative ruling. PLAINTIFF FIRST AMERICAN TITLE INSURANCE COMPANY'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION is Granted as to issues 1, 3, 5, and 9 and moot as to the remaining issues. Defendant cannot establish the causes of action for breach of contract or bad faith or the request for punitive damages. In addition, defendant is not entitled to a declaration that it is owed further policy benefits because the undisputed facts demonstrate the contrary. The undisputed evidence demonstrates that (1) defendant was legally obligated to pay out the undisbursed funds to the contractors; and (2) the undisbursed funds exceeded the amount that plaintiff ultimately paid the contractors. Policy Exclusion 3(a) applies because defendant "created" or "suffered" the lien by failing to pay the undisbursed funds. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. Judge: Harold Kahn; Court Reporter: Sherry Sawyer, CSR # 5976
=(302/HEK)
